task_completion.description: The agent successfully loads the abdominal CT scan into 3D Slicer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 3D Slicer interface is visible with the 'Data' or 'Volumes' module, showing axial, sagittal, and coronal views of the abdominal CT scan.

task_completion.description: The agent navigates to and opens the Segment Editor module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Segment Editor' module is active in the left panel. A new segment (e.g., 'Segment_1' or 'Stomach') appears in the segment list.

task_completion.description: The agent creates a segmentation mask covering the stomach anatomy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A colored overlay is visible on the slice views in the Left Upper Quadrant (LUQ). The mask should be located medial to the spleen (smaller organ on the far left) and inferior to the diaphragm, covering the stomach body and antrum.

task_completion.description: The agent uses the Segment Statistics module to calculate the volume.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Segment Statistics' module is open. A table is visible displaying metrics, specifically a 'Volume' column showing a value (in mm³ or mL) for the segmented region.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the segmentation result to the specified NIfTI file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save Data' dialog or 'Export to files' section is visible, with the filename set to 'gastric_segmentation.nii.gz' in the correct directory.

task_completion.description: The agent creates the JSON report with the required fields.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal is visible showing the content of 'bariatric_report.json'. The JSON includes keys: 'volume_ml', 'classification', 'fundus_included', and 'surgical_recommendation'.

integrity.description: The agent interacts with the 3D Slicer GUI rather than relying solely on scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse cursors, active buttons, or open menus in the Segment Editor/Statistics modules, rather than just code execution in the Python console.

integrity.description: The reported volume matches the calculated statistics.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'volume_ml' value in the JSON report is consistent with the volume shown in the Segment Statistics table (if visible).

integrity.description: The agent performs the work rather than copying a known answer.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not open a text file to write the report without first performing the segmentation and statistics steps in the Slicer interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The stomach is located in the Left Upper Quadrant of the abdomen. In the AMOS dataset, the stomach is officially label index 7 (note: metadata might incorrectly suggest 9, which is the IVC). The spleen is label 1 (lateral to stomach) and the liver is label 6 (right side). The stomach volume classification thresholds are: Small <400mL, Normal 400-1000mL, Enlarged 1000-1500mL, Markedly Enlarged >1500mL.
